bands.label: low
bands.text:
Loud sounds, bright light, or a richly stimulating environment don't cause you much discomfort. You handle intense external stimulation well without feeling overwhelmed.

This gives you resilience in fast-paced, noisy environments where others might tire more quickly.

bands.label: moderate
bands.text:
You're moderately sensitive to sensory stimuli and the emotional atmosphere around you, though you generally handle intense stimulation without significant discomfort.

This gives you a balanced sensitivity that doesn't get in the way of functioning in different environments.

bands.label: elevated
bands.text:
You're noticeably more sensitive than the average person to sensory stimuli — bright light, loud sounds, strong smells — as well as to the emotional atmosphere in a room.

This isn't a flaw — it often comes with deeper processing of information and a fine perception of nuance. Deliberately managing your sensory load (breaks in a quiet place, controlling the intensity of stimuli) helps you use this sensitivity as a resource.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
You're deeply sensitive to sensory and emotional stimuli. A richly stimulating environment — noise, crowds, bright light — quickly leads to a feeling of overload and exhaustion.

This is often linked to uniquely deep information processing, fine aesthetic perception, and high empathy. Planning regular periods of quiet and recovery isn't a luxury — it's a necessary condition for maintaining productivity and emotional balance.

questions.text: Loud sounds or bright light tire me out quickly.
I sharply sense the emotional atmosphere in a room.
Rich sensory experiences quickly overwhelm me.
I notice details that others miss.
I need time alone to recover after an intense day.

portraits.title: The Deep Processor
portraits.text: Your heightened sensitivity to sensory and emotional signals comes with deeper information processing and a fine perception of details others may miss. This is a valuable resource, provided you deliberately manage your own load and regularly recover in a calm environment.

portraits.title: The Resilient Processor
portraits.text: You handle intense sensory and emotional stimulation well without feeling overwhelmed. This gives you resilience in fast-paced, eventful environments.
